vocab.meaning: sự tự ngẫm nhanh chóng/thoáng qua
vocab.example: Occasionally, I use my reflection for a bit of momentary introspection to gather my thoughts before a big meeting.
I only look in the mirror for a quick sartorial adjustment to ensure my tie is straight or my shirt is tucked in.

vocab.meaning: một sự cần thiết mang tính thực tế
vocab.example: Looking in the mirror is merely a practical necessity for me, especially when I’m shaving to avoid any nicks.

vocab.meaning: kiểm tra tư thế/chỉnh đốn lại dáng vẻ
vocab.example: Every now and then, I glance at a mirror to check my alignment and make sure I’m not slouching.

vocab.meaning: hình ảnh phản chiếu từ phía rìa mắt
vocab.example: I rarely stare directly; I usually just catch a peripheral reflection of myself in shop windows while walking.

vocab.meaning: tỏa ra/thể hiện một vẻ ngoài như thế nào đó
vocab.example: I check the mirror to ensure I project an air of professionalism before stepping into the office.

vocab.meaning: sự cân đối của khuôn mặt
vocab.example: I’m not obsessed with facial symmetry, but I do look in the mirror to make sure my hair isn't a total mess.

vocab.meaning: điều chỉnh lại tư thế đứng/ngồi
vocab.example: I find mirrors helpful for mending my posture, as I often forget to keep my back straight during the day.

vocab.meaning: kiểm tra một cách kín đáo/không gây chú ý
vocab.example: I prefer an unobtrusive check in a small pocket mirror rather than staring at a large wall mirror in public.
Mirrors provide essential visual feedback when I’m trying on new clothes to see if the fit is right.
